text:
There was a retired geology professor named Harold who always kept a box of fossils in his study. Every morning he'd open it, examining the ancient treasures, dreaming about their stories, and occasionally trying to identify new species.

One morning, while flipping through a particularly intriguing tooth he hadn't identified yet, Harold heard a strange buzzing noise outside his window. Curious, he went downstairs to investigate. There, on a bush, was a hummingbird so large it seemed impossible.

"Good morning," it said, flashing a big purple feather boa.

Harold blinked. "A hummingbird? But..."

"It's called a patagonita gigantea," it explained proudly. "You've never seen one?"

Harold shook his head. "No..."

"Actually," it continued, hopping closer, "we're pretty rare here. But here's the deal—I want some sugar water. Preferably with a little mint."

Harold, intrigued, obliged. Within minutes the humongous bird
